mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single quarter, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real GDP growth indicate a supply-demand mismatch where capacity utilization falls faster than aggregate demand, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not diverge significantly when INDPRO and GDPC1 move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 310, any increase in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate expansion, forcing a sharp repricing of growth stocks and generating negative forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL above the 310 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as consumption-driven earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST month-over-month changes.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200000 for three consecutive months, it indicates an overheating labor market that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately capping equity upside and producing negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in overheating periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals strong consumer resilience against tight financial conditions, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not systematically rise when RSAFS increases while FEDFUNDS holds steady or declines.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained regime where industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth, capacity bottlenecks drive corporate margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > GDPC1 growth rate
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not diverge significantly when INDPRO and GDPC1 move in opposite directions.
